verbatim_text: It can produce millions and millions of tokens. And as it does so, it keeps improving performance and eventually matches the performance of Gemini 3 Pro tasks. So essentially, it's a really good, effective algorithm, this model QEDnano. So what did we learn here? First, we can represent an algorithm with just four billion parameters. Second, if we use the right techniques, we can train this algorithm effectively to adapt at test time and match the performance of a much bigger model. So how did we get here? And the rest of the talk is sort of about this. It's divided into three parts.
